title: The Furnace Speaks
lines: The bottom of the VEIN, and it is warm. Not geology-warm. Metabolically warm. The Convergence runs hot.
You burned your way down — combustion, fever, a body that spends itself like fuel. Nineteen floors of exothermic argument.
Everything that ever lived here is concentrated in this chamber, and you are raising its temperature just by standing still.
The question beneath every splice: what does a thing become when nothing is forcing it? You answered: faster.
Fire does not descend. It was never supposed to be here. The VEIN built a chimney out of you on the way down, and called it a diver.
What you became will not last — heat never does. It will matter instead. I have recorded the difference.
